FaceID Library

# Update api.rst (remove the problematic Database class reference) @” API Reference =============

FaceID Class

The main class for face recognition operations.

class faceid.core.FaceID(model_type: str = 'facenet', detector_backend: str = 'mediapipe', device: str = 'auto')[source]

Bases: object

Main FaceID class for face recognition and identification

Features: - Face detection in images and video streams - Face encoding/embedding generation - Face matching and identification - Database management for known faces - Real-time recognition

compare_faces(face1: str | ndarray, face2: str | ndarray) → float[source]

Compare two faces and return similarity score

Parameters:

  • face1 – First face image

  • face2 – Second face image

Returns:

Similarity score between 0 and 1

identify_face(image: str | ndarray, threshold: float = 0.6) → List[Dict][source]

Identify faces in an image

Parameters:

  • image – Path to image or numpy array

  • threshold – Similarity threshold for matching (lower = stricter)

Returns:

List of dictionaries with recognition results

register_face(image: str | ndarray, person_id: str, person_name: str = None, metadata: Dict = None) → bool[source]

Register a new face in the database

Parameters:

  • image – Path to image or numpy array

  • person_id – Unique identifier for the person

  • person_name – Name of the person

  • metadata – Additional metadata

Returns:

True if registration successful

train_model(images_dir: str, save_path: str = None) → None[source]

Train or fine-tune the face recognition model

Parameters:

  • images_dir – Directory containing person folders with images

  • save_path – Path to save the trained model

verify_face(image: str | ndarray, person_id: str, threshold: float = 0.6) → Tuple[bool, float][source]

Verify if a face matches a specific person

Parameters:

  • image – Face image to verify

  • person_id – Person ID to verify against

  • threshold – Similarity threshold

Returns:

(is_match, similarity_score)

Methods

register_face(image_path, person_id, person_name)

Register a new face from an image file.

Parameters:

  • image_path – Path to the image file (string)

  • person_id – Unique identifier for the person (string)

  • person_name – Display name for the person (string)

Raises:

Exception – If face not found or registration fails

Example:

face_system.register_face("john.jpg", "john_doe", "John Doe")
identify_face(image_source)

Identify faces in an image or video frame.

Parameters:

image_source – Path to image file or numpy array (video frame)

Returns:

List of dictionaries containing face information

Return type:

list

Example:

results = face_system.identify_face("test.jpg")
for result in results:
    print(result['bbox'], result['matches'])
compare_faces(face1_path, face2_path)

Compare two face images and return similarity score.

Parameters:

  • face1_path – Path to first face image (string)

  • face2_path – Path to second face image (string)

Returns:

Similarity score between 0 and 1

Return type:

float

Example:

similarity = face_system.compare_faces("face1.jpg", "face2.jpg")
if similarity > 0.6:
    print("Faces match!")

Database Management

The face database is managed internally. To list registered people:

people = face_system.database.list_people()
for person in people:
    print(f"{person['name']} (ID: {person['person_id']})")

Utility Functions

draw_bbox(image, bbox, label=None, color=(0, 255, 0))

Draw bounding box on image.

Parameters:

  • image – Input image (numpy array)

  • bbox – Bounding box coordinates [x1,y1,x2,y2]

  • label – Optional label text

  • color – Box color as BGR tuple

Returns:

Image with drawn box

Return type:

numpy.ndarray
